inline_comments.message: NB: we can agree with everybody about the topic name.

same class already thr in cps-ncmp-service.
If possible it can be altogether be brought to cps-service and can be directly used in cps-ncmp-service.

NB: extra DB call just to get the anchor and then later we are fetching dataspace name and anchor name which is readily available.
Check if we can have an overloaded version of your method.
Or a different method that readily accepts the relevant params like anchorname , dataspacename etc.

NB: similar class in NCMP as well.
But I can understand its needed here.
Need to think abt some common code in cps-service layer to be used by both CPS-Core and NCMP.
careful here. have seen observedTimestamp passed as Null in production code as well.

NB: maybe introduce it when needed.
You can add in the commit message abt the functionality as part of this patch. and what is pending.

Otherwise with this TODO , sonar will complain.
changed the topic name as it was used in Montreal release. I think it should be fine
we need to call DB to get Anchor because we need schema set name for notification which is not passed in request parameter.
null and empty check is already handled in rest layer. do we need to validate null again?

maybe for now remove this class from here.
and skip using the time in the cloud event.
when we move this class from cps-ncmp-service to cps-service layer then we can later populate the same.

I think we should not have a separate Application Context.
You can still use an alternate to create the Cloud Builder.
There are examples in the NCMP code that doesnt use the Application Context to fetch the bean.

If we introduce one more application context that will eventually bring in additional complexity for all of us.

not in favor of using a separate ApplicationContext.
Anyways u are just using it to get event publisher , and we already have other ways to get that.

NB: nothing is async here.
can remove this .
sendDataUpdatedEvent should be fine.
Minor issue: +0002 is a timezone offset of 2 minutes, which is a bit unrealistic. Real TZ offsets are usually in 30 minute increments at least, like -0100 or +0530

Can you confirm if this is the expected behaviour?
That it returns UPDATE if it NOT root xpath?
Unit test of publishDataUpdateEvent should be added showing what operation gets published, given different input operations and root/non-root xpaths
for this we need to change the class name too.

not in use now.
can remove it.

you should also check if the exception is actually logged.
otherwise it looks confusing.
added check for validation of log message.
risk here if the delete fails then also we are sending the message.
risk here if the delete fails then also we are sending the message.
NB: I woudl group it with teh other service ie move to line #67
NB: bit unusual name for a build method
Q: should this this not be generated
Q: i thought you were going to do a more fine grained option (per Anchor?) as well, is that a future improvement?

should you have a (unique) separator, maybe even space as that is NOT allowed in either name string...
Do YOu need to split it again? IF so this is important if not just for visibility I woudl insert a '-' or '_'

I understand setCloudEvent is also a build method as per my previous comment why do we have to build methods ?!
I discussed thsi with Priyank and agreed this needs to be addressed
create method and two build methdods ?! I am getting lost...
remove this as this part is it is explained and tested in the next block from line #492
I think you can insert the expected strings here instead of asserting them at lines #59 and #60
use static imports tio short this
I dont understand this test, why done DELET become UPDATe, what does 'non container' mean, I think it needs a better descrption
I would strongly prefer a separate small test for this scenario, that means you can remvoe 2 columns from THIS test and teh scenarios become shorter. You will need no WHERE for the other test
similar convention was used in NcmpCloudEventBuilder.

Here we are generating CloudEvent with specified field values. 
can not be generated
yes, we have planned to commit separately for granular control of notification at anchor level.
changes the test case description. for container nodes, even create and delete operation are supposed to generate UPDATE events.
Yes , create a separate test case

yeah. we event corrected that.
patch got merged yesterday.
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/137734

I think this will be a problem as "_" is a valid character for dataspace/anchor names! We should use one of the unsupported name characters as a separator, e.g. ":" or ";", here is the full list:

    UNSUPPORTED_NAME_CHARACTERS = "!\" #$%&'()*+,./\\:;<=>?@[]^`{|}~"
I would suggest to use : # if no other objections.
